The user shared the log output. I can see there's an error:

```
00:43:58: [StationpediaPlus] Error in ChangeDisplay_Postfix: Object reference not set to an instance of an object
```

This is happening when trying to add the Special Behaviors section. The error occurs because something is null - likely `item.SubObject` or one of the layout components we're trying to add.

Looking at the screenshot, it shows only one behavior is displaying, and only the description text (the title "IC10 Execution" is overlapping with the description). The layout is quite messed up.

The issue is that we're trying to use SPDAListItem which has a fixed layout - the SubObject, SubText etc. might not be configured the way we expect for paragraph text.

Let me think about what's null:
1. The `item.SubObject` - might be null
2. Adding VerticalLayoutGroup when one already exists might cause issues
3. The ContentSizeFitter might be conflicting

Since the text is showing but overlapping, the layout group approach is breaking things. Let me try a simpler approach - just append the text to the page description like before, but we could make it scrollable, OR we could try to create our own custom UI elements from scratch.

Actually, the cleanest solution might be to:
1. Skip the complex layout modifications
2. Use the existing Description field and append formatted text there
3. Or create a simple text-only approach

Let me revert to the simple description append approach since that worked fine - it just wasn't a dropdown. The user asked for a dropdown, but the dropdown approach has layout issues.

Let me try a middle ground - use the category dropdown but just put a single TextMeshProUGUI in it instead of multiple list items.I see the error - there's a null reference when adding the ContentSizeFitter/LayoutGroup components. The layout is getting messed up. Let me try a cleaner approach - create a single text block inside the category dropdown instead of trying to hack the SPDAListItem layout: Build succeeded. This new approach:

1. Creates the collapsible dropdown header ("Special Behaviors" with the arrow)
2. Inside, creates a **single TextMeshProUGUI** component from scratch
3. All behaviors are formatted as one continuous text block with:
   - Gold colored titles (`<color=#FFD700>`)
   - Regular gray text for descriptions
   - Line breaks between entries
4. Uses `ContentSizeFitter` to auto-expand the height based on content
5. Word wrapping is enabled so paragraphs flow properly

Close the game and restart to test. This should give you a clean dropdown that expands to show full paragraph text for all behaviors.

The user is saying the Special Behaviors section gets cut off and they can't scroll down to see more. Also the scroll icon disappeared. The problem is likely that:

1. The category's Contents might need a VerticalLayoutGroup or something to properly size itself based on content
2. The category itself might need to be added to some layout system
3. The ContentSizeFitter on the text isn't propagating up to the parent category properly

Let me look at how existing categories work in the game. The category has a `Contents` RectTransform that holds child items. The issue is that our dynamically created text element isn't properly sizing the category container.

Looking at the screenshot, it shows "IC10 Execution" with text and then "Chip Reset Behavior" is getting cut off at the bottom. The scroll bar is also missing.

The problem might be that:
1. The category.Contents needs to have proper height set or auto-calculated
2. The whole page Content needs to know about this new element

Let me try adding a LayoutElement to the category itself, and also ensure the Contents has a VerticalLayoutGroup. Also need to force a layout rebuild.

Actually, looking at the code more carefully - the issue is that we're adding the text to `category.Contents`, but the Contents' size isn't being updated properly. We need to:

1. Add a VerticalLayoutGroup to category.Contents if it doesn't exist
2. Add ContentSizeFitter to category.Contents to auto-size based on children
3. Possibly add LayoutElement to the category itself
4. Force a layout rebuild

Let me also check if I need to call some Unity layout rebuild functions.The category's content area isn't properly resizing. We need to add layout components to the category itself and force a layout rebuild. Let me fix this: Build compiled successfully (the DLL is in `bin\`), but can't copy because game is running. Close the game and restart.

Changes made:
1. Added `VerticalLayoutGroup` to `category.Contents` - this tells the layout system how to arrange children
2. Added `ContentSizeFitter` to `category.Contents` - so it auto-sizes based on the text height  
3. Added `LayoutElement` to the text object for proper layout participation
4. Called `LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate()` on both the contents and parent - this forces Unity to recalculate all sizes immediately

This should fix the scrolling/cut-off issue by making the category properly report its actual size to the parent scroll view.

_**User (2025-12-28 08:52Z)**_

CAn we implement this so I dont have to restart the game every time? 


Hot-reloading mods (harmony patches) for faster development cycles.

Initial setup:
Install the ScriptEngine mod in BepInEx/plugins: https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx.Debug/releases/tag/r11
Set HideManagerGameObject = true in BepInEx/config/BepInEx.cfg
Put your mod (.dll and .pdb file) into BepInEx/scripts instead of BepInEx/plugins
Make sure that your mod does properly clean up all harmony patches in the OnDestroy method.
public class YourPlugin : BaseUnityPlugin
{
    private Harmony _harmony;

    private void Awake()
    {
        _harmony = new Harmony(PluginGuid);
        _harmony.PatchAll();
    }

    private void OnDestroy()
    {
        _harmony.UnpatchSelf();
    }
}


On update:
Build the mod and overwrite .dll and .pdb in BepInEx/scripts
Hit F6 key in-game to trigger the reload

Note: You can change the behavior of reloading (load on start, reload automatically on file change, etc.) in BepInEx/config/com.bepis.bepinex.scriptengine.cfg 


---

---

The user wants to implement hot-reloading support for faster development. The key is to properly clean up Harmony patches in OnDestroy so that when the mod is reloaded via ScriptEngine (F6), it doesn't leave orphaned patches.

Looking at the current code, I can see:
1. The mod already has a `_harmony` field
2. It patches in `ApplyHarmonyPatches()` 
3. There's an `OnDestroy()` method but it only cleans up the tooltip background texture

I need to add `_harmony.UnpatchSelf()` to the OnDestroy method.

Also, I should note that this mod uses StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersMods, not BepInEx directly. The ScriptEngine approach is for BepInEx plugins. However, the principle is the same - we need to clean up Harmony patches properly on destroy.

Let me update the OnDestroy method to unpatch Harmony.Good idea! We need to add Harmony cleanup to `OnDestroy`. Let me add that: Build compiled successfully (DLL is in `bin\`). Now for the ScriptEngine hot-reload setup:

**To use hot-reloading:**

1. **Download ScriptEngine** from https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx.Debug/releases/tag/r11
   - Get `ScriptEngine_vX.X.zip`
   - Extract to `rocketstation_Data\StreamingAssets\BepInEx\plugins\`

2. **Edit BepInEx config** (`BepInEx/config/BepInEx.cfg`):
   ```ini
   [Caching]
   HideManagerGameObject = true
   ```

3. **Create scripts folder**: `BepInEx/scripts/`

4. **Copy your mod there** after building:
   - `StationpediaPlus.dll` 
   - `StationpediaPlus.pdb` (for debugging)

5. **In-game**: Press **F6** to reload the mod

The `OnDestroy` now properly:
- Calls `_harmony.UnpatchSelf()` to remove all our Harmony patches
- Clears static state (tooltip, DeviceDatabase, Instance)
- Cleans up textures

**Note**: Your mod uses StationeersLaunchPad, not BepInEx directly. ScriptEngine is designed for BepInEx plugins. You might need to create a separate lightweight BepInEx wrapper plugin that loads/unloads the StationeersLaunchPad mod, or test if ScriptEngine works with your current setup. The key thing is the Harmony cleanup is now in place for whatever hot-reload mechanism you end up using.

---

_**User (2025-12-28 08:57Z)**_

So: that cleans up your Harmony Patches, but not the stuff you added to SP. Any GameObjects you create will remain. You must manually clean those up.
Otherwise it's a memory leak

---

The user is right - when we create UI elements like the Special Behaviors category dropdown, those GameObjects persist even after the mod is destroyed. We need to track and clean up any GameObjects we create.

Looking at the code, in `ChangeDisplay_Postfix` we create:
1. A `StationpediaCategory` (instantiated from categoryPrefab)
2. A `GameObject` with TextMeshProUGUI inside it

We add the category to `__instance.CreatedCategories` which the game should clean up on page change, but if the mod is destroyed mid-session, those might leak.

Also, our tooltip components (`SPDALogicTooltip`, `SPDASlotTooltip`, etc.) are added to existing game objects - those should be cleaned up too.

Let me add:
1. A static list to track all created GameObjects
2. Cleanup in OnDestroy to destroy those GameObjects
3. Also need to clean up the tooltip components we add to existing objectsYou're right! We need to track and clean up the GameObjects we create (the Special Behaviors category) and the tooltip components we add. Let me fix that: Now I need to track the GameObjects and components when we create them. Let me find where we add tooltip components: I need to track these added components. Let me update those methods to add tracking: Now let me find where we create the Special Behaviors category and track that: I see the code adds the category to `__instance.CreatedCategories` which the game manages. But we also need to track our own GameObjects and any components we add (like VerticalLayoutGroup and ContentSizeFitter on Contents). Let me track those too: Now let's build: The fields are private but I'm accessing them from a nested class. Let me make them internal: Build succeeded. Now the mod tracks all created GameObjects and components, and cleans them up in `OnDestroy`. 

Summary of cleanup tracking:
- **Tooltip components** (`SPDALogicTooltip`, `SPDASlotTooltip`, `SPDAVersionTooltip`, `SPDAMemoryTooltip`) are added to `_addedComponents`
- **Special Behaviors text GameObject** is added to `_createdGameObjects`
- **Special Behaviors category GameObject** is added to `_createdGameObjects`
- **VerticalLayoutGroup** and **ContentSizeFitter** (if we add them) are added to `_addedComponents`

On `OnDestroy`:
1. First destroys all added components
2. Then destroys all created GameObjects
3. Then cleans up Harmony patches
4. Finally cleans up static references

_**User (2025-12-28 17:41Z)**_

ScriptEngine
Loads and reloads BepInEx plugins from the BepInEx\scripts folder. User can reload all of these plugins by pressing the keyboard shortcut defined in the config. Shortcut is F6 by default.
Very useful for quickly developing plugins as you don't have to keep reopening the game to see your changes.

Remember to clean up after the old plugin version in case you need to. Things like harmony patches or loose GameObjects/MonoBehaviours remain after the plugin gets destroyed. Loose gameobjects and monobehaviours in this case are objects that are not attached to the parent scriptengine gameobject. For example:

private static Harmony _hi;

private void Awake()
{
    _hi = Harmony.CreateAndPatchAll(typeof(Hooks));
    LoadYourResources();
}

private void OnDestroy()
{
    _hi?.UnpatchSelf();
    UnloadYourResources();
}
How to use: This is a plugin. Put the compiled DLL into BepInEx/plugins.

Can you make sure we are cleaning up everything when I hot reload the plugin so we don't get errors?

The user wants to ensure proper cleanup for hot-reload with BepInEx ScriptEngine. Let me review what we're currently doing and make sure everything is properly cleaned up.

Key things to clean up:
1. Harmony patches - we have this
2. Created GameObjects - we're tracking these
3. Added components - we're tracking these
4. Static references
5. Any event subscriptions or callbacks

Let me read the current OnDestroy and make sure it's complete.Let me review our current cleanup implementation: Now let me see our current OnDestroy: The OnDestroy looks comprehensive but let me also check the field declarations to see what else might need cleanup: I see an issue - we're calling `DontDestroyOnLoad(gameObject)` which might interfere with ScriptEngine's cleanup. Also we need to reset the static lists when re-initializing. Let me also check if there's a coroutine running that needs to be stopped: There are coroutines being started. Let me also look at one of those tooltip components with coroutines: The tooltip components manage their own coroutines. The main issues I see are:

1. `DontDestroyOnLoad` - this will keep the GameObject alive across scene loads, but ScriptEngine should be destroying it on reload
2. Static lists need to be re-initialized on reload (not just cleared)
3. `_tooltipStyle` and `_stylesInitialized` need reset
4. Stop any running coroutines on the main mod

Let me make these improvements: Build succeeded and copied. The cleanup now includes:

1. **`StopAllCoroutines()`** - Stops any running coroutines on the main mod
2. **Harmony patches** - `_harmony.UnpatchSelf()` 
3. **Added components** - Destroys all tooltip components we added to game UI (wrapped in try-catch)
4. **Created GameObjects** - Destroys Special Behaviors categories and text objects (wrapped in try-catch)
5. **Static list reinitialization** - Creates new empty lists for next load
6. **GUI resources** - Destroys `_tooltipBackground` texture, nulls `_tooltipStyle`, resets `_stylesInitialized`
7. **Tracking state** - Resets `_lastPageKey`, `_stationpediaFound`, `_nextCheckTime`
8. **Static references** - Clears `Instance`, `DeviceDatabase`, `GenericDescriptions`
9. **Removed `DontDestroyOnLoad`** - Let ScriptEngine manage our lifecycle

Press F6 in-game to hot-reload. Check the console for cleanup messages like:
```
[StationpediaPlus] OnDestroy called - cleaning up for hot-reload...
[StationpediaPlus] Unpatching Harmony patches...
[StationpediaPlus] Cleaning up X added components...
[StationpediaPlus] Cleaning up X created GameObjects...
[StationpediaPlus] Mod destroyed and cleaned up successfully
```

{

  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nBepInEx;\r\nusing HarmonyLib;\r\nusing StationeersMods.Interface;\r\nusing UnityEngine;\r\nusing UnityEngine.EventSystems;\r\nusing Newtonsoft.Json;\r\n\r\nnamespace StationpediaPlus\r\n{\r\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n    /// BepInEx plugin wrapper for ScriptEngine hot-reload support.\r\n    /// This allows the mod to be loaded/reloaded via F6 during development.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    [BepInPlugin(\"com.stationpediaplus.mod\", \"StationpediaPlus\", \"1.0.0\")]\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusPlugin : BaseUnityPlugin\r\n    {\r\n        private StationpediaPlusMod _modInstance;\r\n        \r\n        void Awake()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] BepInEx plugin Awake - creating mod instance...\");\r\n            \r\n            // Create a new GameObject for our mod (or attach to this one)\r\n            _modInstance = gameObject.AddComponent&lt;StationpediaPlusMod&gt;();\r\n            \r\n            // Initialize the mod manually since we're not going through StationeersLaunchPad\r\n            _modInstance.InitializeFromBepInEx();\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        void OnDestroy()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] BepInEx plugin OnDestroy\");\r\n            // The StationpediaPlusMod's OnDestroy will handle cleanup\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "BepInEx;\r\nusing HarmonyLib;\r\nusing StationeersMods.Interface;\r\nusing UnityEngine;\r\nusing UnityEngine.EventSystems;\r\nusing Newtonsoft.Json;\r\n\r\nnamespace StationpediaPlus\r\n{\r\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n    /// BepInEx plugin wrapper for ScriptEngine hot-reload support.\r\n    /// This allows the mod to be loaded/reloaded via F6 during development.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    [BepInPlugin(\"com.stationpediaplus.mod\", \"StationpediaPlus\", \"1.0.0\")]\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusPlugin : BaseUnityPlugin\r\n    {\r\n        private StationpediaPlusMod _modInstance;\r\n        \r\n        void Awake()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] BepInEx plugin Awake - creating mod instance...\");\r\n            \r\n            // Create a new GameObject for our mod (or attach to this one)\r\n            _modInstance = gameObject.AddComponent&lt;StationpediaPlusMod&gt;();\r\n            \r\n            // Initialize the mod manually since we're not going through StationeersLaunchPad\r\n            _modInstance.InitializeFromBepInEx();\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        void OnDestroy()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] BepInEx plugin OnDestroy\");\r\n            // The StationpediaPlusMod's OnDestroy will handle cleanup\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n",

The user is saying F6 doesn't do anything. ScriptEngine needs to be installed properly. Let me check a few things:

1. ScriptEngine needs to be in BepInEx/plugins, not scripts
2. The DLL we're building might need to be a proper BepInEx plugin for ScriptEngine to load it

Wait, looking at the ScriptEngine docs again:
- ScriptEngine itself is a plugin that goes in BepInEx/plugins
- It loads/reloads plugins from BepInEx/scripts folder

So the user needs:
1. ScriptEngine.dll in BepInEx/plugins
2. Our mod in BepInEx/scripts

But our mod is using StationeersLaunchPad's ModBase, not BepInEx's BaseUnityPlugin. ScriptEngine might only work with BepInEx plugins.

Let me check if ScriptEngine is actually installed, and if our mod needs to be a BepInEx plugin for ScriptEngine to work with it.ScriptEngine itself needs to be installed as a BepInEx plugin. Let me check if it's there: ScriptEngine is installed. Let me check if our mod is actually in the scripts folder and if the scripts folder exists: The DLL is there. The issue is likely that our mod uses `ModBase` (StationeersLaunchPad), not `BaseUnityPlugin` (BepInEx). ScriptEngine only loads BepInEx plugins.

Let me check ScriptEngine's config to see if there's a hotkey setting: The config looks fine (F6 is the key). The issue is that **ScriptEngine only loads BepInEx plugins** (classes that extend `BaseUnityPlugin`), but our mod extends `ModBase` from StationeersLaunchPad.

You have two options:

**Option 1:** Enable `LoadOnStart = true` and restart the game (won't give you hot-reload, but will load from scripts folder)

**Option 2:** Create a BepInEx wrapper for the mod for hot-reload during development

Let me create a simple BepInEx plugin wrapper that will work with ScriptEngine: I'll add a BepInEx plugin class that can be used with ScriptEngine. This will be a separate entry point that coexists with the ModBase for StationeersLaunchPad: Now I need to add the `InitializeFromBepInEx` method to the mod class: Now let's build and copy: The build succeeded but the copy to the mods folder failed (game is running). The DLL is in the bin folder - let me copy it to the scripts folder: Done! Now you need to also copy the `descriptions.json` file to the scripts folder since the mod loads it from the same directory as the DLL: Now press **F6** in-game. It should:

1. Unload the old plugin (calling `OnDestroy`)
2. Load the new plugin from the scripts folder
3. Show console messages like:
   - `[StationpediaPlus] BepInEx plugin Awake - creating mod instance...`
   - `[StationpediaPlus] InitializeFromBepInEx called`
   - `[StationpediaPlus] Mod initialized via BepInEx`

**Note:** If the mod is also loading via StationeersLaunchPad (from the mods folder), you might get double loading. For pure hot-reload testing, you could temporarily move the mod out of the `My Games\Stationeers\mods\StationpediaPlus` folder.

---

Let me look at the ScriptEngine source code to understand how it works and why F6 might not be working.I see the issue! ScriptEngine checks `ReloadKey.Value.IsDown()` which uses BepInEx's `KeyboardShortcut`. Let me check if this is working properly. The key thing I notice is:

1. It looks for `BaseUnityPlugin` types in the DLL
2. It checks `if (!typeof(BaseUnityPlugin).IsAssignableFrom(type)) continue;`

Our code should work since we added a `BaseUnityPlugin` class. Let me check the BepInEx logs to see what's happening. First, let me verify the scripts folder path: The folder is named `Scripts` with capital S! But ScriptEngine uses lowercase `scripts`:

```csharp
public string ScriptDirectory => Path.Combine(Paths.BepInExRootPath, "scripts");
``` Windows is case-insensitive for paths, so it sees them as the same. The folder should work either way on Windows. Let me check the BepInEx log to see if ScriptEngine is even running: I see ScriptEngine is loaded (`Loading [Script Engine 11.0]`), but there's no message about pressing F6 or reloading. The log shows the game was started but F6 wasn't pressed (or didn't register).

Let me check if the scripts folder actually has the DLL and if it's a valid BepInEx plugin: The files are there. Let me verify our DLL actually exports the BepInEx plugin class properly by checking the assembly: Can't test that way. Let me look at the actual game log instead - check if pressing F6 generates any output: The log file shows this session didn't have any reload attempts. The log gets cleared on game start. 

Here's what to check:

1. **Is the game currently running?** If so, press F6 now and let's check the log again.

2. **Is something else capturing F6?** Some games or overlays might capture the key.

3. **Is the game window focused?** F6 only works when the game window is in focus.

Try this: While in-game with the game window focused, press F6 and then let's check the log file again. You can also try enabling `LoadOnStart = true` in the config to test if the plugin loads at all: I've enabled `LoadOnStart = true` in the ScriptEngine config. Now:

1. **Restart the game**
2. Check if the plugin loads automatically on start
3. Look for `[StationpediaPlus]` messages in the game console or BepInEx log

This will tell us if the plugin DLL itself is valid and loadable by ScriptEngine. If it loads on start but F6 doesn't work, then it's a keybinding issue. If it doesn't load on start, there might be an issue with our plugin class.

_**User (2025-12-28 17:58Z)**_

Might be an issue with the plugin class loading from that /scripts folder? I am not seeing it load. Normally the mod has to load in through SLP remember?

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="copilot_replaceString">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **copilot_replaceString**</summary>

Additional data:

```json
{
  "vscodeToolId": "copilot_replaceString",
  "isHidden": true
}
```
Result:

```json
{
  "invocationMessage": "Using \"Replace String in File\"",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nfor ScriptEngine hot-reload support (F6).\r\n    /// This is a standalone plugin that doesn't depend on StationeersLaunchPad at runtime.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    [BepInPlugin(\"com.stationpediaplus.dev\", \"StationpediaPlus-Dev\", \"1.0.0\")]\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusPlugin : BaseUnityPlugin\r\n    {\r\n        private StationpediaPlusCore _core;\r\n        \r\n        void Awake()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus-Dev] BepInEx plugin Awake\");\r\n            _core = gameObject.AddComponent&lt;StationpediaPlusCore&gt;();\r\n            _core.Initialize();\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        void OnDestroy()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus-Dev] BepInEx plugin OnDestroy\");\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n    /// StationeersLaunchPad mod wrapper - loads when installed as a proper mod.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusMod : ModBehaviour\r\n    {\r\n        private StationpediaPlusCore _core;\r\n        \r\n        public override void OnLoaded(ContentHandler contentHandler)\r\n        {\r\n            base.OnLoaded(contentHandler);\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] OnLoaded via StationeersLaunchPad\");\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        void Awake()\r\n        {\r\n            // Check if core is already running (from BepInEx plugin)\r\n            if (StationpediaPlusCore.Instance != null)\r\n            {\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] Core already running from BepInEx, skipping SLP initialization\");\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] SLP Awake - creating core\");\r\n            _core = gameObject.AddComponent&lt;StationpediaPlusCore&gt;();\r\n            _core.Initialize();\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n    /// Core mod functionality - shared between BepInEx and SLP entry points.\r\n    /// This MonoBehaviour contains all the actual mod logic.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusCore : Mono\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "for ScriptEngine hot-reload support (F6).\r\n    /// This is a standalone plugin that doesn't depend on StationeersLaunchPad at runtime.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    [BepInPlugin(\"com.stationpediaplus.dev\", \"StationpediaPlus-Dev\", \"1.0.0\")]\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusPlugin : BaseUnityPlugin\r\n    {\r\n        private StationpediaPlusCore _core;\r\n        \r\n        void Awake()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus-Dev] BepInEx plugin Awake\");\r\n            _core = gameObject.AddComponent&lt;StationpediaPlusCore&gt;();\r\n            _core.Initialize();\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        void OnDestroy()\r\n        {\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus-Dev] BepInEx plugin OnDestroy\");\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n    /// StationeersLaunchPad mod wrapper - loads when installed as a proper mod.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusMod : ModBehaviour\r\n    {\r\n        private StationpediaPlusCore _core;\r\n        \r\n        public override void OnLoaded(ContentHandler contentHandler)\r\n        {\r\n            base.OnLoaded(contentHandler);\r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] OnLoaded via StationeersLaunchPad\");\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        void Awake()\r\n        {\r\n            // Check if core is already running (from BepInEx plugin)\r\n            if (StationpediaPlusCore.Instance != null)\r\n            {\r\n                ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] Core already running from BepInEx, skipping SLP initialization\");\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            ConsoleWindow.Print(\"[StationpediaPlus] SLP Awake - creating core\");\r\n            _core = gameObject.AddComponent&lt;StationpediaPlusCore&gt;();\r\n            _core.Initialize();\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /// &lt;summary&gt;\r\n    /// Core mod functionality - shared between BepInEx and SLP entry points.\r\n    /// This MonoBehaviour contains all the actual mod logic.\r\n    /// &lt;/summary&gt;\r\n    public class StationpediaPlusCore : Mono",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```
Status: **completed**


</details>
</tool-use>

---

The user is right - our mod uses StationeersLaunchPad (SLP) which has its own loading mechanism via ModBehaviour/ModBase. The BepInEx plugin wrapper I added might not be enough because:

1. ModBehaviour inherits from something that SLP provides
2. The DLL might have dependencies on SLP that aren't available when ScriptEngine tries to load it
3. ScriptEngine might be failing silently when trying to load our DLL

Let me check the BepInEx log to see if there are any errors when ScriptEngine tries to load our plugin. The issue is likely that:
1. Our DLL references StationeersMods.Interface (SLP)
2. When ScriptEngine tries to load it, it can't resolve that dependency
3. It fails silently or logs an error

Let me check the log for errors and also think about whether we can make a standalone BepInEx plugin that doesn't depend on SLP for hot-reload purposes.You're right! The problem is our DLL depends on `StationeersMods.Interface` (SLP), and when ScriptEngine tries to load it, it can't resolve that dependency because it's not in the scripts folder.

Let me check the log for errors: It's trying to load but probably failing. Let me check if there's a way to see more detailed logs. Looking at the ScriptEngine code, it catches exceptions but logs them. Let me check after the "Loading plugins from" line: I see it says `Loading plugins from ...StationpediaPlus.dll` but then nothing - no success message like `Loading [StationpediaPlus 1.0.0]`. The ScriptEngine is silently failing to find a valid `BaseUnityPlugin` type.

The issue is our class inherits from `ModBehaviour` which requires SLP. ScriptEngine uses `typeof(BaseUnityPlugin).IsAssignableFrom(type)` to find plugins, but since our `StationpediaPlusPlugin` class references `StationpediaPlusMod` which extends `ModBehaviour`, the type loading fails.

We have two options:

**Option 1:** Create a separate, minimal BepInEx-only plugin DLL just for hot-reload testing

**Option 2:** Copy the SLP DLL to the scripts folder as a dependency

Let me try Option 2 first - it's simpler: Now restart the game and check if ScriptEngine can load the plugin. The log should show `Loading [StationpediaPlus 1.0.0]` if it finds the BepInEx plugin class.

Actually, looking at this more carefully - the real issue is that our plugin class references `StationpediaPlusMod` which inherits from `ModBehaviour`. When ScriptEngine tries to enumerate types, it needs to load the full type hierarchy.

A cleaner approach would be to create a **completely standalone BepInEx plugin** that doesn't inherit from ModBehaviour at all - just for development/hot-reload. Let me refactor this: The problem is that even the `StationpediaPlusPlugin` class references `StationpediaPlusMod` which inherits from `ModBehaviour`. When ScriptEngine tries to load our assembly and iterate types, it fails because `ModBehaviour` comes from `StationeersMods.Interface`.

Let me try a different approach - we can use conditional compilation or just make the main class work as both a BepInEx plugin AND an SLP mod. Let me refactor to make `StationpediaPlusMod` inherit from `BaseUnityPlugin` directly for hot-reload, with a wrapper for SLP: Now I need to update the class body to use `StationpediaPlusCore` instead of `StationpediaPlusMod`: I see there's some leftover code. Let me fix the class properly - remove the old `OnLoaded` and `InitializeFromBepInEx` since those are now handled by the wrapper classes: Now I need to update all references from `StationpediaPlusMod` to `StationpediaPlusCore` in the rest of the file. Let me find them: I need to replace all `StationpediaPlusMod` references with `StationpediaPlusCore` (except for line 43 which is the class declaration). Let me do a bulk replacement in the file: Now let's build and see if there are any errors: The file is locked by a previous PowerShell command that loaded the assembly. Let me copy from the obj folder instead: Now restart the game with `LoadOnStart = true` and check if the BepInEx plugin loads. The key difference now:

1. **`StationpediaPlusPlugin`** - Pure BepInEx plugin, doesn't reference `ModBehaviour` directly
2. **`StationpediaPlusMod`** - SLP wrapper that checks if core is already running
3. **`StationpediaPlusCore`** - Actual mod logic as a plain `MonoBehaviour`

But wait - `StationpediaPlusPlugin` still references `StationpediaPlusCore`, and `StationpediaPlusMod` (the SLP wrapper) might still cause issues when ScriptEngine tries to load the assembly. The issue is that ScriptEngine calls `ass.GetTypes()` which tries to load ALL types, including `StationpediaPlusMod` which requires `ModBehaviour`.

The real solution is to **not include the SLP wrapper class** when building for ScriptEngine. But that's complex. Let me check if copying the SLP Interface DLL to scripts folder helps - ScriptEngine adds the scripts folder to the assembly resolver: Good, `StationeersMods.Interface.dll` is there. Now when you restart the game with `LoadOnStart = true`, it should be able to resolve the `ModBehaviour` type from that DLL.

Restart the game and check the BepInEx log to see if `[StationpediaPlus-Dev]` appears (that's the new BepInEx plugin name).

The user is showing me the game log, and I notice that our StationpediaPlus mod is NOT appearing in the log at all! Neither the BepInEx plugin version nor the SLP version is loading.

Let me check what happened to the file - there was a note that some edits were made to StationpediaPlus.cs. I should check the current state and also check if there are build errors.

The last terminal output showed Exit Code: 1 which means the build failed. Let me check the current state of the file and fix any issues.I see StationpediaPlus isn't appearing at all in the log - not even an error. The build also failed (Exit Code: 1). Let me check the current state of the file and fix the build: The file is locked. The code compiled successfully (no syntax errors), but the bin folder DLL is locked by PowerShell. Let me delete the bin folder and rebuild: There's another PowerShell process (51132) holding the file. Let me build to a different output folder: There's a bug in the path. Let me just copy directly from obj: Good, that worked. Now also check the BepInEx log to see what happened with ScriptEngine: Interesting! ScriptEngine tried to load `StationeersMods.Interface.dll` as a plugin (it shouldn't - that's just a library). But I don't see it trying to load `StationpediaPlus.dll`.

Looking at the ScriptEngine code again:
```csharp
foreach (string path in Directory.GetFiles(ScriptDirectory, "*.dll", ...))
    LoadDLL(path, scriptManager);
```

It should load all DLLs. Let me check what's in the scripts folder: Both DLLs are there. The log shows ScriptEngine DID load `StationeersMods.Interface.dll`, but then it didn't load `StationpediaPlus.dll` - probably because the Interface DLL doesn't have a BepInEx plugin in it (no `BaseUnityPlugin` class) so it silently continues.

The problem is: It loaded the DLLs in alphabetical order, so `StationeersMods.Interface.dll` came BEFORE `StationpediaPlus.dll`. ScriptEngine scans DLLs for `BaseUnityPlugin` types and that DLL has none so it just... finishes?

Wait, looking more carefully at the log - after loading Interface.dll, it moves to the next line: `[Info   :   BepInEx] Loading [Stationpedia Extractor 1.0.0]` - that's a different plugin entirely. It seems ScriptEngine only tried to load Interface.dll.

Let me check if the files are properly enumerated. The issue might be that the `.dll` files are being checked but only Interface is listed because of sorting/timing... Actually I see the log shows only ONE file being loaded from scripts.

This is a race condition or the script engine is having an issue with the Interface DLL. Let me remove the Interface DLL and try again - ScriptEngine adds the plugins folder to its resolver anyway: Now restart the game. With `LoadOnStart = true`, ScriptEngine should only find `StationpediaPlus.dll` and try to load it.

But wait - there's still the fundamental problem: our DLL references `ModBehaviour` from `StationeersMods.Interface`. When ScriptEngine tries to enumerate types, it will try to resolve that type...

Actually, looking at ScriptEngine's code again:
```csharp
defaultResolver.AddSearchDirectory(ScriptDirectory);
defaultResolver.AddSearchDirectory(Paths.ManagedPath);
defaultResolver.AddSearchDirectory(Paths.BepInExAssemblyDirectory);
```

It should find Interface.dll in `BepInEx\plugins\StationeersLaunchPad\`. Let me check: It's there. But `BepInExAssemblyDirectory` might not include subdirectories. Let me check what that path resolves to:

Looking at ScriptEngine, it only adds `BepInExAssemblyDirectory` (which is `BepInEx/core`) not the plugins directory with its subdirectories.

The simplest fix is to just let SLP load the mod normally. For hot-reload, the ScriptEngine approach is complex because of the dependencies. 

**Let's go back to basics**: The game log you showed doesn't have StationpediaPlus loading through SLP either! It should show `loading the 'StationpediaPlus' mod`. That's the real issue.

Let me check if the mod folder is properly set up: The mod files are there. The problem is likely the mod isn't enabled in the game's mod manager, OR the DLL is broken/incomplete.

Let me check the About folder for the mod.xml: The About.xml looks fine. Let's check if the mod has an assembly definition that SLP looks for: The structure looks correct. The issue is the DLL is likely the old version that still has errors or it's locked. Let me copy the fresh one from obj: Also let me make sure the mod is enabled. Check the settings.xml or mods list: No results - the mod might not be in the enabled mods list! Let me check the mod list in settings: The mod list might be in a different file. Let me check SLP's mod tracking: **There it is!** StationpediaPlus is in the config but `Enabled="false"`! The replacement didn't work (XML multiline). Let me fix it more directly: Now it's `Enabled="true"`. **Restart the game** and the mod should load through StationeersLaunchPad. You should see `loading the 'StationpediaPlus' mod` in the game console.

For ScriptEngine hot-reload, we can revisit that later - but first let's make sure the basic SLP loading works.

_**User (2025-12-28 18:04Z)**_

---

_**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Ah, my bad! The user had SLP loading working before but I suggested trying ScriptEngine which led us down a rabbit hole. Now they've re-enabled it and it works.

So the situation is:
1. SLP loading works fine
2. ScriptEngine hot-reload doesn't work because of the ModBehaviour dependency issue

Let me acknowledge this and ask what they want to do next - continue with the mod development using SLP (restarting game each time), or try to fix the ScriptEngine hot-reload setup.Got it, sorry for the confusion! SLP loading works fine. The ScriptEngine hot-reload was a side quest that ran into dependency issues.

**Options going forward:**

1. **Continue with SLP** - Just restart the game to test changes. Simple and reliable.

2. **Enable auto-reload** - Set `EnableFileSystemWatcher = true` in ScriptEngine config. When you copy a new DLL to scripts folder, it auto-reloads (but still has the dependency issue).

3. **Fix ScriptEngine** - Would need to either:
   - Build a separate minimal BepInEx-only DLL without the `ModBehaviour` class
   - Or add the SLP plugins folder to ScriptEngine's resolver (modify ScriptEngine source)

For now, I'd recommend sticking with **Option 1** (SLP) since it works. The hot-reload complexity isn't worth it unless you're making very frequent changes.

Want me to set up a quick build script that copies to the mods folder so testing is just: build → restart game?

---

The user wants me to modify ScriptEngine to work with SLP mods. Let me think about this from first principles.

The problem is:
1. ScriptEngine loads DLLs from the scripts folder
2. It needs to resolve dependencies (like StationeersMods.Interface.dll)
3. Currently it only adds these directories to the resolver:
   - ScriptDirectory (scripts folder)
   - Paths.ManagedPath (game's Managed folder)
   - Paths.BepInExAssemblyDirectory (BepInEx/core)

It doesn't add the plugins folder or its subdirectories, so it can't find StationeersMods.Interface.dll which is in BepInEx/plugins/StationeersLaunchPad/

Let me read the ScriptEngine source carefully and figure out what modifications are needed.Let me analyze the ScriptEngine source thoroughly: Now I understand the architecture. The issues are:

1. **Assembly resolver** - Only searches `scripts`, `Managed`, and `BepInEx/core`. Doesn't include `BepInEx/plugins` or subdirectories.

2. **Only loads `BaseUnityPlugin`** - Ignores classes that extend `ModBehaviour` (SLP's base class).

3. **No SLP integration** - Doesn't call SLP's loading mechanism.

Let me modify ScriptEngine to support SLP mods: Now let me update the `ReloadPlugins` method to clean up SLP mods: Now the critical part - update `LoadDLL` to add the plugins directory (including subdirectories) to the resolver, and to also load SLP `ModBehaviour` classes: Now let's build the modified ScriptEngine: This is an older .NET Framework 3.5 project using packages.config. Let me check if NuGet packages need to be restored: It needs .NET Framework 3.5. Let me try using MSBuild directly or check if there's a solution: Need to restore packages with NuGet. Let me try the solution file: The packages folder doesn't exist. Need to restore with nuget.exe: Packages restored. Now let's build: Excellent! Build succeeded! Now let's copy the new ScriptEngine to the game's plugins folder: Now let's revert our StationpediaPlus mod back to a simpler form - just the `ModBehaviour` class with an `Initialize()` method that ScriptEngine will call: Actually, the current structure should work - the ScriptEngine will now look for both `BaseUnityPlugin` and `ModBehaviour` classes. But we have both in our DLL. Let me simplify - since ScriptEngine now supports `ModBehaviour` directly, we can just use that: Now update the rest of the class to use `StationpediaPlusMod` instead of `StationpediaPlusCore`: Now let me check if there's proper Initialize method and OnLoaded: Good, but we need to add back the `OnLoaded` method for SLP support. Let me check further: The OnLoaded was removed. Let me add it back: Now build and copy: Old PowerShell still has a lock. Let me copy from obj: Now here's the summary of what we've done:

## Modified ScriptEngine Changes

1. **Added plugins directory to assembly resolver** - Now searches `BepInEx/plugins` and all subdirectories so it can find `StationeersMods.Interface.dll`

2. **Added SLP ModBehaviour detection** - The `IsModBehaviourType()` method checks if a type inherits from `ModBehaviour`

3. **Loads ModBehaviour classes** - When a ModBehaviour is found, it:
   - Adds it as a component to the ScriptEngine GameObject
   - Calls the `Initialize()` method if it exists
   - Tracks the instance for cleanup

4. **Cleanup on reload** - Before reloading, destroys all tracked SLP mod instances (which triggers their `OnDestroy`)

## StationpediaPlus Changes

- Simplified back to a single `StationpediaPlusMod` class extending `ModBehaviour`
- Has both `OnLoaded()` (for SLP) and `Initialize()` (for ScriptEngine hot-reload)
- `OnDestroy()` handles all cleanup (Harmony, components, GameObjects)

**Now restart the game.** When it starts:
1. SLP will load the mod from the mods folder normally
2. ScriptEngine with `LoadOnStart = true` might also try to load from scripts folder

After the game is running, press **F6** to test hot-reload. You should see:
- `[Script Engine] Destroying SLP mod: StationpediaPlusMod`
- `[StationpediaPlusMod] OnDestroy called...` (cleanup messages)
- `[Script Engine] Loading SLP mod: StationpediaPlusMod`  
- `[StationpediaPlusMod] Initialize called`

  },
  "terminalCommandOutput": {
    "text": "\u001b[36m|Console|Logging\" \u001b[90m-Context \u001b[97m1\u001b[90m,\u001b[97m1\r\n\r\n\r\n\u001b[0m&gt; ## If enabled, hides BepInEx Manager GameObject from Unity.\r\n  ## This can fix loading issues in some games that attempt to prevent BepInEx from being loaded.\r\n\r\n&gt; [Logging]\r\n\r\n&gt; ## Enables showing unity log messages in the BepInEx logging system.\r\n  # Setting type: Boolean\r\n\r\n&gt; ## If enabled, writes Standard Output messages to Unity log\r\n&gt; ## NOTE: By default, Unity does so automatically. Only use this option if no console messages are visible in Unity log\r\n  ##\r\n  # Default value: false\r\n&gt; LogConsoleToUnityLog = false\r\n\r\n&gt; [Logging.Console]\r\n\r\n&gt; ## Enables showing a console for log output.\r\n  # Setting type: Boolean\r\n  # Default value: false\r\n&gt; Enabled = false\r\n\r\n&gt; ## If enabled, will prevent closing the console (either by deleting the close button or in other platform-specific way).\r\n  # Setting type: Boolean\r\n\r\n&gt; ## If true, console is set to the Shift-JIS encoding, otherwise UTF-8 encoding.\r\n  # Setting type: Boolean\r\n\r\n&gt; ## Hints console manager on what handle to assign as StandardOut. Possible values:\r\n&gt; ## Auto - lets BepInEx decide how to redirect console output\r\n&gt; ## ConsoleOut - prefer redirecting to console output; if possible, closes original standard output\r\n&gt; ## StandardOut - prefer redirecting to standard output; if possible, closes console out\r\n  ##\r\n&gt; # Setting type: ConsoleOutRedirectType\r\n  # Default value: Auto\r\n&gt; # Acceptable values: Auto, ConsoleOut, StandardOut\r\n  StandardOutType = Auto\r\n\r\n&gt; ## Which log levels to show in the console output.\r\n  # Setting type: LogLevel\r\n\r\n&gt; [Logging.Disk]\r\n\r\n  # Default value: true\r\n&gt; Enabled = true\r\n\r\n\r\n&gt; ## If enabled, BepInEx will save patched assemblies into BepInEx/DumpedAssemblies.\r\n  ## This can be used by developers to inspect and debug preloader patchers.\r\n\r\n&gt; ## If enabled, BepInEx will load patched assemblies from BepInEx/DumpedAssemblies instead of memory.\r\n  ## This can be used to be able to load patched assemblies into debuggers like dnSpy.\r\n\r\n&gt; ## If enabled, BepInEx will call Debugger.Break() once before loading patched assemblies.\r\n  ## This can be used with debuggers like dnSpy to install breakpoints into patched assemblies before they are loaded.\r\n\r\n\u001b[?1004h",

---

The user is trying to test the ScriptEngine hot-reload functionality. They disabled loading the mod through SLP (Stationeers Mod Loader) and tried pressing F6, but nothing happened.

Let me think about this:

1. ScriptEngine watches the `BepInEx/scripts` folder for DLLs
2. When F6 is pressed, it should reload DLLs from that folder
3. The user has the DLL in the scripts folder (we copied it there)

The issue might be:
1. ScriptEngine isn't loading at all
2. ScriptEngine is loading but not finding the DLL
3. ScriptEngine needs the mod to NOT be loaded by SLP first (which is what the user tried)
4. There might be configuration issues

Let me check the ScriptEngine configuration and see if there's a LoadOnStart setting or something. Also, I should check if there are any logs to see what's happening.

Actually, the key question is: is ScriptEngine even detecting the DLL? Let me check:
1. Is ScriptEngine enabled? We enabled it in modconfig.xml earlier
2. Is the DLL actually in the scripts folder?
3. Does ScriptEngine have any logging we can check?

The user should be able to see console output if BepInEx console is enabled. Let me check the BepInEx config to make sure the console is visible, and also check if there's any way to see what ScriptEngine is doing.

I should also verify:
1. The scripts folder exists and has the DLL
2. ScriptEngine.dll is in the plugins folder
3. BepInEx console is enabled so we can see debug output

Let me check these things.Let me check a few things to diagnose why F6 isn't working: The BepInEx console is disabled! That's why you can't see any output. Let me enable it and also check the log file for what happened: I can see ScriptEngine loaded and it's loading the DLL at startup:
```
[Info   :Script Engine] Loading plugins from C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers\BepInEx\scripts\StationpediaPlus.dll
```

But I don't see any message about what types it found or loaded. Let me check if there were any errors. Also, let me look at the full log for any ScriptEngine-related messages: So ScriptEngine says it's "Loading plugins from" but doesn't log what it found. This was a game startup. Let me check if there's any config for ScriptEngine and see what the hotkey is actually set to: The config looks good - `LoadOnStart = true` and `ReloadKey = F6`. Let me enable the BepInEx console so you can see what's happening, and also add more logging to ScriptEngine: That regex didn't work. Let me fix it directly: Good, the console is now enabled. Now let's look at the ScriptEngine code again - I want to add logging to see if it's actually detecting the ModBehaviour type: The code looks good. Let me add some additional logging to see what types are being found. But first, let me check if the issue is that F6 is actually being detected. Let me add very explicit logging: Now let me rebuild ScriptEngine: Now I've:
1. **Enabled BepInEx console** - you'll see a console window when the game starts
2. **Added more logging to ScriptEngine** - it will now log when F6 is pressed and show all types it finds in the assembly

**To test:**
1. Start the game fresh
2. You should see a **BepInEx console window** pop up
3. Look for `[Script Engine] Loading plugins from...` messages on startup
4. When in-game, press **F6**
5. Look for `[Script Engine] Reload key pressed! Reloading plugins...`

If you don't see the "Reload key pressed" message when pressing F6, then the keypress isn't being detected (possibly a Unity input issue or F6 is bound to something else in the game).

**About your question:** No, you should NOT need to load the mod from SLP. The whole point of the ScriptEngine modification is to detect and load ModBehaviour classes directly from the scripts folder, without SLP being involved in loading them. ScriptEngine should:
1. Find `StationpediaPlus.dll` in the scripts folder
2. Detect that `StationpediaPlusMod` inherits from `ModBehaviour`
3. Instantiate it and call `Initialize()`

Let me know what the console shows when you start the game and press F6!

The user is showing me the game log output. I notice there's no mention of ScriptEngine loading plugins or pressing F6. The log shows various mods loading via SLP (StationeersLaunchPad) but nothing about ScriptEngine detecting the F6 key press.

Let me look at what's happening:
1. The BepInEx console is now enabled (since they're seeing this output)
2. The mod loading is happening through SLP, not ScriptEngine
3. There's no "[Script Engine]" messages in this log at all

Wait - I don't see any BepInEx log messages in this output. This looks like the Unity/game console output (Player.log), not the BepInEx console. The BepInEx console would show messages like "[Info   :Script Engine]".

Let me check the BepInEx LogOutput.log to see what ScriptEngine is doing. But actually the user should be seeing a separate BepInEx console window if it's enabled.

The key issue here is that there's no ScriptEngine output visible. Either:
1. The BepInEx console isn't showing (maybe not enabled properly?)
2. ScriptEngine isn't logging anything
3. The user is looking at the wrong log

I should ask them to check:
1. Is there a separate BepInEx console window?
2. What does the BepInEx LogOutput.log show?
3. Did they press F6 and see any response?

Actually, looking more carefully - this log doesn't show any BepInEx log format (like "[Info:..."). This is the Unity Player.log format. The BepInEx console would have different formatting.

Let me ask them to check the BepInEx log file or if they see the console window.This log is from the Unity Player.log, not the BepInEx console. I don't see any ScriptEngine messages here. Let me check the BepInEx log to see what's happening: The log shows ScriptEngine loaded the DLL at startup but I don't see:
1. The "Scanning assembly for types..." message I added
2. Any "Reload key pressed!" message when you pressed F6

This means **the updated ScriptEngine.dll wasn't actually loaded** - the game is still using the old version! This is because you need to restart the game after I copied the new ScriptEngine.dll.

**The issue**: The ScriptEngine.dll was copied while the game was running, but the game was already using the old version in memory. 

**To test properly:**
1. Close the game completely
2. Start the game again fresh
3. The BepInEx log should now show the new logging messages like "Scanning assembly for types..."
4. Then press F6 in-game

Also, did you see a **separate BepInEx console window** pop up when the game started? It should be a black command-prompt style window separate from the game window.

The user says there's no BepInEx console window popping up, but the SLP (StationeersLaunchPad) log does. This is strange because I thought I enabled the BepInEx console.

Let me check the BepInEx log file again to see what's happening when F6 is pressed. Also, let me verify the console setting actually got applied.Let me check if the console setting actually took effect: The console is enabled. Let me check the fresh BepInEx log file: This is great progress! Look at the log:

```
[Info   :Script Engine] Loading plugins from C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers\BepInEx\scripts\StationpediaPlus.dll
[Info   :Script Engine] Scanning assembly for types...
[Info   :Script Engine] Loading SLP mod: StationpediaPlusMod
[Message:Script Engine] Reloaded all plugins!
```

**The mod is being loaded on startup!** ScriptEngine successfully:
1. Found the DLL
2. Scanned for types
3. Detected `StationpediaPlusMod` as an SLP mod
4. Loaded it

However, I don't see "Reload key pressed!" in the log, which means F6 isn't being detected. This could be:
1. The game is intercepting F6 before Unity gets it
2. There's a conflict with another keybinding

Let me also check if `Initialize()` or `Called Initialize()` appears - I don't see it in the log, which means the Initialize method might not be getting called. Let me check the StationpediaPlus code: The code looks good. The issue is that on startup, the mod is loaded but `Initialize()` is only called via the delayed coroutine, and I don't see "Called Initialize() on StationpediaPlusMod" in the log. Also, F6 press isn't being detected.

Let me check if there's something blocking the key detection. Let me try a different approach - maybe the key polling isn't working correctly. Let me also check if F6 is maybe being used by the game: Hmm, only one hash was returned. Let me check both files individually: Good, the files match. Now looking at the log again:

```
[Info   :Script Engine] Loading SLP mod: StationpediaPlusMod
[Message:Script Engine] Reloaded all plugins!
```

I see it says "Loading SLP mod" but I don't see "Called Initialize() on StationpediaPlusMod" which should appear after that. This means the delayed coroutine might be running but there's an issue.

Wait - looking more carefully at my ScriptEngine code, there's an issue! The `DelayAction` coroutine runs on the NEXT frame, but by then the log message has already printed "Reloaded all plugins!". The initialization happens asynchronously. Let me check if `Initialize()` is actually being called - look for the output from that method in the SLP console or Unity log.

Actually, the bigger issue is: **why isn't F6 working for reloading?**

Let me check if the Update method is even running by adding more logging: Let me add some debug logging to understand if Update() is even being called: Now restart the game and:
1. Load into a world
2. Wait 30+ seconds 
3. Then check the BepInEx log file for messages like "ScriptEngine Update running"

This will tell us if the ScriptEngine's Update() is being called at all. The log will also show Debug-level messages now (the Debug logging level should show them in the log file even if not in the console).

Also - since you mentioned the SLP log pops up, let me check if StationpediaPlus is actually initializing via SLP or ScriptEngine. When you start the game and get to the main menu, what do you see in the SLP console about StationpediaPlus?

The user is saying that:
1. StationpediaPlus works fine (loaded by ScriptEngine on startup)
2. They unchecked it in SLP console so SLP isn't loading it (good - avoids double loading)
3. But F6 still doesn't work for hot-reload

Let me check the BepInEx log to see if the Update() debug messages are appearing.Good, that's the right approach - let ScriptEngine load it, not SLP. Now let's check if the Update() is running and debug the F6 issue: I see the log still doesn't have my debug messages about "ScriptEngine Update running" - the log was written before the updated ScriptEngine was used. Did you restart the game after I last updated ScriptEngine?

The log timestamp shows the game is from before the latest update. Let me verify: The DLL (10:20:12) is newer than the log (10:18:55), which means the log is from before the latest ScriptEngine build. 

**Can you restart the game and:**
1. Get into a world  
2. Wait ~30 seconds
3. Press F6 a few times
4. Exit the game

Then I'll check the log again to see if:
- The "ScriptEngine Update running" debug message appears (confirms Update is being called)
- The "Reload key pressed!" message appears (confirms F6 detection)

This is done from inside of Chainloader's Awake, therefore not all plugis might be loaded yet. BepInDependency attributes are ignored.\"));\n            ReloadKey = Config.Bind(\"General\", \"ReloadKey\", new KeyboardShortcut(KeyCode.F6), new ConfigDescription(\"Press this key to reload all the plugins from the scripts folder\"));\n            QuietMode = Config.Bind(\"General\", \"QuietMode\", false, new ConfigDescription(\"Disable all logging except for error messages.\"));\n            IncludeSubdirectories = Config.Bind(\"General\", \"IncludeSubdirectories\", false, new ConfigDescription(\"Also load plugins from subdirectories of the scripts folder.\"));\n            EnableFileSystemWatcher = Config.Bind(\"AutoReload\", \"EnableFileSystemWatcher\", false, new ConfigDescription(\"Watches the scripts directory for file changes and automatically reloads all plugins if any of the files gets changed (added/removed/modified).\"));\n            AutoReloadDelay = Config.Bind(\"AutoReload\", \"AutoReloadDelay\", 3.0f, new ConfigDescription(\"Delay in seconds from detecting a change to files in the scripts directory to plugins being reloaded. Affects only EnableFileSystemWatcher.\"));\n            DumpAssemblies = Config.Bind&lt;bool&gt;(\"AutoReload\", \"DumpAssemblies\", false, \"If enabled, BepInEx will save patched assemblies &amp; symbols into BepInEx/ScriptEngineDumpedAssemblies.\\nThis can be used by developers to inspect and debug plugins loaded by ScriptEngine.\");\n\n            if (Directory.Exists(DumpedAssembliesPath))\n                Directory.Delete(DumpedAssembliesPath, true);\n\n            if (LoadOnStart.Value)\n                ReloadPlugins();\n\n            if (EnableFileSystemWatcher.Value)\n                StartFileSystemWatcher();\n            \n            Logger.Log(LogLevel.Info, $\"ScriptEngine initialized. _**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is asking me to step back and think about this more carefully. Let me think about what SLP (StationeersLaunchPad) actually is and how ScriptEngine should work with it.

SLP (StationeersLaunchPad) is indeed a BepInEx plugin that:
1. Loads mods from a separate "mods" folder structure
2. Has its own mod loading system that uses `ModBehaviour` as the base class
3. ModBehaviour inherits from MonoBehaviour

ScriptEngine is a BepInEx plugin that:
1. Loads DLLs from the BepInEx/scripts folder
2. Was designed to reload BepInEx plugins (BaseUnityPlugin) on F6
3. I modified it to also detect and load ModBehaviour classes

The problem we're hitting is that ScriptEngine's Update() isn't being called. Why?

Actually wait - looking at the log again:
```
[Info   :   BepInEx] Loading [Script Engine 10.0]
[Info   :Script Engine] Loading plugins from C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers\BepInEx\scripts\StationpediaPlus.dll
[Info   :Script Engine] Scanning assembly for types...
[Info   :Script Engine] Loading SLP mod: StationpediaPlusMod
[Message:Script Engine] Reloaded all plugins!
```

This shows the Awake() method IS running (because it calls ReloadPlugins() on startup which produces those messages). But Update() never logs anything.

Why would Update() not be called on a BaseUnityPlugin?

BaseUnityPlugin inherits from MonoBehaviour. BepInEx creates a GameObject and attaches the plugin to it. For Update() to be called:
1. The GameObject must be active
2. The MonoBehaviour must be enabled
3. The game must be running (not paused in a way that stops Update)
 
I'm investigating a discrepancy between the log messages and expected plugin behavior. The build context suggests the old version might still be in use, which complicates debugging the Update() method's non-execution. The core issue seems to revolve around how BepInEx manages plugin lifecycle and GameObject activation.

Two potential approaches emerge for addressing the hot-reload challenge:

First, we could modify ScriptEngine to operate independently, directly detecting and instantiating ModBehaviour classes. However, this risks bypassing SLP's critical infrastructure like ContentHandler.

Alternatively, we might leverage SLP's existing mod loading mechanism. This approach would involve either triggering SLP to reload a specific mod or utilizing any built-in reload functionality within the plugin system.

The key challenge is understanding how to seamlessly integrate ScriptEngine's hot-reload capabilities with SLP's established mod management framework.

The core user requirement involves a straightforward workflow: modify StationpediaPlus.cs, build the DLL, press F6 in-game, and instantly reload the updated mod. This demands three critical components: F6 detection, old mod unloading, and new mod initialization.

A promising strategy might involve embedding hot-reload key detection directly within StationpediaPlus itself, potentially circumventing the current limitations of ScriptEngine's Update() method.

By leveraging the existing Update() method that already tracks page changes, we could implement a targeted approach: detect F6, reload JSON descriptions, and manage Harmony patching. While this technique wouldn't support full code hot-reloading, it would enable dynamic data updates.

Alternative strategies for comprehensive code reloading might include file watching mechanisms, inter-plugin reload triggers, or exploring coroutine-based key polling techniques.

The fundamental question remains whether ScriptEngine's core functionality is universally operational, suggesting a deeper investigation into potential systemic loading issues.

The proposed solution focuses on a straightforward implementation: integrating F6 detection directly within StationpediaPlus, enabling runtime description refreshes without complete mod restarts. This method specifically supports updating textual content, configuration parameters, and behavioral descriptions efficiently.You're right, let me step back and think about this properly.

**What is SLP?**
SLP (StationeersLaunchPad) is indeed a BepInEx plugin that:
1. Provides its own mod loading system on top of BepInEx
2. Uses `ModBehaviour` as the base class (which extends MonoBehaviour)
3. Provides `OnLoaded(ContentHandler)` for mod initialization
4. Has its own mod folder structure and loading lifecycle

**The Real Problem**
ScriptEngine was designed to reload **BepInEx plugins**, not SLP mods. I've been trying to hack it to also load ModBehaviour classes, but:
1. We're bypassing SLP's ContentHandler
2. ScriptEngine's Update() isn't even being called (possibly a BepInEx/Unity issue with this game)
3. It's fighting against two different mod systems

**A Simpler Approach**
What if we don't use ScriptEngine at all? Your StationpediaPlus mod's `Update()` IS running (it detects page changes). We could add F6 handling directly to the mod:

1. **Keep SLP loading the mod normally** (enable it in SLP)
2. **Add F6 detection to StationpediaPlus.Update()**
3. **On F6 press:**
   - Reload `descriptions.json` from disk
   - Unpatch and re-patch Harmony (if needed)
   - Clear cached tooltips

This won't hot-reload C# code changes (you'd still restart for those), but it WOULD hot-reload:
- All description text changes
- Logic type tooltips
- Special behaviors content
- Any JSON data

Since most of your iteration is on descriptions content, this might be exactly what you need. Want me to implement this simpler approach?

3. **StationpediaPlus** is an SLP mod (ModBehaviour), not a BepInEx plugin

The fundamental problem: ScriptEngine's Update() isn't being called. This is strange because ScriptEngine IS a BepInEx plugin (BaseUnityPlugin which extends MonoBehaviour), and it should get Unity lifecycle callbacks.

- ScriptEngine is loading
- ScriptEngine is loading the DLL from scripts folder
- But no Update messages

The issue might be that ScriptEngine as a BepInEx plugin doesn't have Update() being called because BepInEx plugins are attached to a special GameObject that may not run Update in the same way.

Actually, I should check - does ScriptEngine work at ALL for regular BepInEx plugins? The user said "F6 doesn't do anything" - that suggests even the original ScriptEngine functionality (reloading BepInEx plugins) isn't working.

Let me think about what options we have:

1. **Fix ScriptEngine** - Figure out why Update() isn't being called. This might be a Unity/BepInEx issue specific to this game.

2. **Alternative hot-reload approach** - Use file system watcher instead of key press. ScriptEngine already has this option (`EnableFileSystemWatcher`). If we enable that, it will auto-reload when files change, no F6 needed.

3. **Build a proper SLP hot-reload system** - Since SLP is already running and its mods' Update() works, we could potentially have SLP itself handle reloading.
 
I'm intrigued by the file system watcher option. This could potentially circumvent the Update() problem entirely. If enabled, it would automatically detect and reload scripts when files change, eliminating the need for manual key presses. This might be a straightforward solution to the current hot-reload limitations.

The configuration seems promising - a simple toggle could provide an automatic reload mechanism that doesn't rely on problematic Update() calls. I'll investigate how to activate this feature and test its reliability.You're right. Let me think about this differently.

**The Core Problem**: ScriptEngine's `Update()` isn't being called, so F6 detection fails. But ScriptEngine has another option - `EnableFileSystemWatcher` which uses a `FileSystemWatcher` instead of polling in Update().

Let me check if that's set up to work: There's `EnableFileSystemWatcher` - this uses a .NET FileSystemWatcher (NOT Update()) to detect when files in the scripts folder change. Let me enable it and test: Wait - but the FileSystemWatcher callback still needs to trigger a reload, which happens in `ReloadPlugins()`. The issue is that even though FileSystemWatcher runs on a background thread, the actual reload needs to happen on the main thread via `shouldReload` flag which is checked in... `Update()`.

So that won't help either if Update isn't running.

The coroutine approach didn't work either. Let me check the log to see if the coroutine even started.The coroutine started! "KeyCheckCoroutine started" is in the log. But F6 still doesn't trigger reload.

This means the coroutine IS running, but `ReloadKey.Value.IsDown()` is never returning true. The issue is with how BepInEx's `KeyboardShortcut.IsDown()` works - it might require being called from a proper Unity lifecycle method.

Let me try using raw Unity input instead: Close the game and restart. Now it uses `Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.F6)` directly instead of the BepInEx KeyboardShortcut wrapper.

---

_**User (2025-12-28 18:40Z)**_

I know that other people have had this script engine work in stationeers. Are people here aware of the ScriptEngine plugin of BepInEx for hot-reloading mods? It's just sooo good to rebuild the mod, hit F6 in-game and it just works, no more game restarting! 
Are people here aware of the ScriptEngine plugin of BepInEx for hot-reloading mods? It's just sooo good to rebuild the mod, hit F6 in-game and it just works, no more game restarting! Just used it for the IC10 Editor, code only, harmony patches.
I am not sure about prefabs/xml since they are loaded early on game start.
For more complex mods it could make sense to put the "logic/patches" in a separate dll and just reload this one.
I will write a short description in the modding-resources channel


Hot-reloading mods (harmony patches) for faster development cycles.

Initial setup:
Install the ScriptEngine mod in BepInEx/plugins: https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx.Debug/releases/tag/r11
Set HideManagerGameObject = true in BepInEx/config/BepInEx.cfg
Put your mod (.dll and .pdb file) into BepInEx/scripts instead of BepInEx/plugins
Make sure that your mod does properly clean up all harmony patches in the OnDestroy method.
public class YourPlugin : BaseUnityPlugin
{
    private Harmony _harmony;

    private void Awake()
    {
        _harmony = new Harmony(PluginGuid);
        _harmony.PatchAll();
    }

    private void OnDestroy()
    {
        _harmony.UnpatchSelf();
    }
}


On update:
Build the mod and overwrite .dll and .pdb in BepInEx/scripts
Hit F6 key in-game to trigger the reload

Note: You can change the behavior of reloading (load on start, reload automatically on file change, etc.) in BepInEx/config/com.bepis.bepinex.scriptengine.cfg 


---

{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/BepInEx.Debug-r11/src/ScriptEngine/ScriptEngine.cs)",
  "pastTenseMessage": "Created [](file:///c%3A/Dev/12-17-25%20Stationeers%20Respawn%20Update%20Code/BepInEx.Debug-r11/src/ScriptEngine/ScriptEngine.cs)",
  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "using BepInEx;using BepInEx.Bootstrap;using BepInEx.Configuration;using BepInEx.Logging;using Mono.Cecil;using System;using System.Collections;using System.Collections.Generic;using System.IO;using System.Linq;using System.Reflection;using System.Text;using Common;using HarmonyLib;using UnityEngine;namespace ScriptEngine{    [BepInPlugin(GUID, \"Script Engine\", Version)]    public class ScriptEngine : BaseUnityPlugin    {        public const string GUID = \"com.bepis.bepinex.scriptengine\";        public const string Version = Metadata.Version;        public string ScriptDirectory =&gt; Path.Combine(Paths.BepInExRootPath, \"scripts\");        private GameObject scriptManager;        private ConfigEntry&lt;bool&gt; LoadOnStart { get; set; }        private ConfigEntry&lt;KeyboardShortcut&gt; ReloadKey { get; set; }        private ConfigEntry&lt;bool&gt; QuietMode { get; set; }        private ConfigEntry&lt;bool&gt; EnableFileSystemWatcher { get; set; }        private ConfigEntry&lt;bool&gt; IncludeSubdirectories { get; set; }        private ConfigEntry&lt;float&gt; AutoReloadDelay { get; set; }        private ConfigEntry&lt;bool&gt; DumpAssemblies { get; set; }        private static readonly string DumpedAssembliesPath = Utility.CombinePaths(Paths.BepInExRootPath, \"ScriptEngineDumpedAssemblies\");        private FileSystemWatcher fileSystemWatcher;        private bool shouldReload;        private float autoReloadTimer;        private void Awake()        {            LoadOnStart = Config.Bind(\"General\", \"LoadOnStart\", false, new ConfigDescription(\"Load all plugins from the scripts folder when starting the application. This is done from inside of Chainloader's Awake, therefore not all plugis might be loaded yet. BepInDependency attributes are ignored.\"));            ReloadKey = Config.Bind(\"General\", \"ReloadKey\", new KeyboardShortcut(KeyCode.F6), new ConfigDescription(\"Press this key to reload all the plugins from the scripts folder\"));            QuietMode = Config.Bind(\"General\", \"QuietMode\", false, new ConfigDescription(\"Disable all logging except for error messages.\"));            IncludeSubdirectories = Config.Bind(\"General\", \"IncludeSubdirectories\", false, new ConfigDescription(\"Also load plugins from subdirectories of the scripts folder.\"));            EnableFileSystemWatcher = Config.Bind(\"AutoReload\", \"EnableFileSystemWatcher\", false, new ConfigDescription(\"Watches the scripts directory for file changes and automatically reloads all plugins if any of the files gets changed (added/removed/modified).\"));            AutoReloadDelay = Config.Bind(\"AutoReload\", \"AutoReloadDelay\", 3.0f, new ConfigDescription(\"Delay in seconds from detecting a change to files in the scripts directory to plugins being reloaded. Affects only EnableFileSystemWatcher.\"));            DumpAssemblies = Config.Bind&lt;bool&gt;(\"AutoReload\", \"DumpAssemblies\", false, \"If enabled, BepInEx will save patched assemblies &amp; symbols into BepInEx/ScriptEngineDumpedAssemblies.\\nThis can be used by developers to inspect and debug plugins loaded by ScriptEngine.\");            if (Directory.Exists(DumpedAssembliesPath))                Directory.Delete(DumpedAssembliesPath, true);            if (LoadOnStart.Value)                ReloadPlugins();            if (EnableFileSystemWatcher.Value)                StartFileSystemWatcher();        }        private void Update()        {            if (ReloadKey.Value.IsDown())            {                ReloadPlugins();            }            else if (shouldReload)            {                autoReloadTimer -= Time.unscaledDeltaTime;                if (autoReloadTimer &lt;= .0f)                    ReloadPlugins();            }        }        private void ReloadPlugins()        {            shouldReload = false;            if (scriptManager != null)            {                if (!QuietMode.Value) Logger.Log(LogLevel.Info, \"Unloading old plugin instances\");                foreach (var previouslyLoadedPlugin in scriptManager.GetComponents&lt;BaseUnityPlugin&gt;())                {                    var metadataGUID = previouslyLoadedPlugin.Info.Metadata.GUID;                    if (Chainloader.PluginInfos.ContainsKey(metadataGUID))                        Chainloader.PluginInfos.Remove(metadataGUID);                }                Destroy(scriptManager);            }            scriptManager = new GameObject($\"ScriptEngine_{DateTime.Now.Ticks}\");            DontDestroyOnLoad(scriptManager);            var files = Directory.GetFiles(ScriptDirectory, \"*.dll\", IncludeSubdirectories.Value ? SearchOption.AllDirectories : SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly);            if (files.Length &gt; 0)            {                foreach (string path in Directory.GetFiles(ScriptDirectory, \"*.dll\", IncludeSubdirectories.Value ? SearchOption.AllDirectories : SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))                    LoadDLL(path, scriptManager);                if (!QuietMode.Value)                    Logger.LogMessage(\"Reloaded all plugins!\");            }            else            {                if (!QuietMode.Value)                    Logger.LogMessage(\"No plugins to reload\");            }        }        private void LoadDLL(string path, GameObject obj)        {            var defaultResolver = new DefaultAssemblyResolver();            defaultResolver.AddSearchDirectory(ScriptDirectory);            defaultResolver.AddSearchDirectory(Paths.ManagedPath);            defaultResolver.AddSearchDirectory(Paths.BepInExAssemblyDirectory);            if (!QuietMode.Value)                Logger.Log(LogLevel.Info, $\"Loading plugins from {path}\");            using (var dll = AssemblyDefinition.ReadAssembly(path, new ReaderParameters {                AssemblyResolver = defaultResolver,                ReadSymbols = true            }))            {                dll.Name.Name = $\"{dll.Name.Name}-{DateTime.Now.Ticks}\";                Assembly ass;                if (DumpAssemblies.Value)                {                    // Dump assembly &amp; load it from disk                    if (!Directory.Exists(DumpedAssembliesPath))                        Directory.CreateDirectory(DumpedAssembliesPath);                       string assemblyDumpPath = Path.Combine(DumpedAssembliesPath, dll.Name.Name + Path.GetExtension(dll.MainModule.Name));                    using (FileStream outFileStream = new FileStream(assemblyDumpPath, FileMode.Create))                    {                        dll.Write((Stream)outFileStream, new WriterParameters()                        {                            WriteSymbols = true                        });                    }                    ass = Assembly.LoadFile(assemblyDumpPath);                    if (!QuietMode.Value)                        Logger.Log(LogLevel.Info, $\"Loaded dumped Assembly from {assemblyDumpPath}\");                } else                {                    // Load from memory                    using (var ms = new MemoryStream())                    {                        dll.Write(ms);                        ass = Assembly.Load(ms.ToArray());                    }                }                foreach (Type type in GetTypesSafe(ass))                {                    try                    {                        if (!typeof(BaseUnityPlugin).IsAssignableFrom(type)) continue;                        var metadata = MetadataHelper.GetMetadata(type);                        if (metadata == null) continue;                        if (!QuietMode.Value)                            Logger.Log(LogLevel.Info, $\"Loading {metadata.GUID}\");                        if (Chainloader.PluginInfos.TryGetValue(metadata.GUID, out var existingPluginInfo))                            throw new InvalidOperationException($\"A plugin with GUID {metadata.GUID} is already loaded! ({existingPluginInfo.Metadata.Name} v{existingPluginInfo.Metadata.Version})\");                        var typeDefinition = dll.MainModule.Types.First(x =&gt; x.FullName == type.FullName);                        var pluginInfo = Chainloader.ToPluginInfo(typeDefinition);                        StartCoroutine(DelayAction(() =&gt;                        {                            try                            {                                // Need to add to PluginInfos first because BaseUnityPlugin constructor (called by AddComponent below)                                // looks in PluginInfos for an existing PluginInfo and uses it instead of creating a new one.
